first_protocol_candidate.reason: Smallest testable bridge over existing static public data and seed snapshot files without old/insecure protocol implementation.

rejected_first_protocol_candidates.reason: Useful later but should be a projection within the local static HTTP prototype, not a separate first protocol.
Useful later but requires the same path traversal and allowlist controls as local static HTTP.
Insecure transport and credential confusion make it unsuitable for the first prototype.
Complex write semantics and client expectations require a later threat model.
Platform permission and credential risks require separate review.
Legacy protocol candidate requiring lab-host review.
Export-root and LAN trust risks require separate review.
Research protocol only; not first implementation.
Depends on future native client implementation and private-data lifecycle review.
Depends on future snapshot consumer implementation and signature/authenticity policy.

next_recommended_milestone: Rust Local Index Parity Candidate v0 only after Cargo availability and review
alternative_next_milestones: Relay Prototype Implementation v0 only after explicit human approval
Windows 7 WinForms Native Skeleton Implementation v0 only after explicit human approval
notes: Relay Prototype Planning v0 is planning only.

No relay runtime, socket listener, protocol server, native sidecar, snapshot mount, live backend proxy, live probe, private file server, download surface, installer action, telemetry, or account behavior is implemented.
The first future prototype should be local-only, read-only, static, allowlisted, and explicitly approved before implementation.
